questions.question: What are the three failure modes of resizing every image to a fixed square?
options: Slow upload, slow download, slow decode
Squished text and faces from distortion, destroyed chart labels and OCR content from downscaling, and (with padding instead) 50%+ of tokens wasted on pad at quadratic attention cost
Color shifts, compression artifacts, EXIF loss
None — square resize is lossless
questions.explanation: Receipts are 1:3, documents portrait, screenshots 9:19.5, medical scans 4096px. The pre-2024 square-resize default threw away exactly the signal OCR and document understanding need.

questions.question: How does NaViT's patch-n'-pack handle a batch of different-sized images?
options: Pads every image to the largest
Each image keeps its native patch grid; all patches concatenate into one long sequence with a block-diagonal mask so each image's patches attend only within their own image
Processes images one at a time
Center-crops everything to match
questions.explanation: Three images of 576 + 256 + 768 tokens become one 1600-token sequence, no padding, no wasted compute. NaViT also introduced fractional patch dropping (50%) for regularization and speed, inherited by SigLIP 2.

questions.question: How does LLaVA-NeXT's AnyRes work, and what is its cost?
options: It retrains the encoder per aspect ratio
Tile the image into a best-fit grid of 336px crops plus a global thumbnail, encode each through the frozen encoder — effective, but a 1344px image at 4x4 costs ~9,800 tokens, most of an 8k context
It vectorizes the image as SVG
It sends multiple resolutions and votes
questions.explanation: AnyRes is the pragmatic route when your encoder is frozen at one resolution: local detail from tiles plus global context from the thumbnail — paid for in token count that explodes with image size.

questions.question: What does Qwen2-VL's M-RoPE give each patch?
options: A unique random id
A 3D position (temporal, height, width) handled by rotary embeddings — native dynamic resolution with no position table, no retraining, any HxW at inference
An aspect-ratio tag in the prompt
A learned resolution embedding per size
questions.explanation: Feed any image; the embedder produces H/14 × W/14 tokens, each rotated by its (t, row, col) position. Qwen2.5/3-VL continue it; InternVL3's V2PE is the same idea with per-modality encoding.

questions.question: Which any-resolution approach fits which constraint?
options: They are interchangeable in all cases
AnyRes when the encoder is frozen at one resolution; NaFlex/NaViT packing when training the encoder for one checkpoint serving every aspect ratio; M-RoPE when you control the architecture end to end
Always M-RoPE regardless of constraints
Always tile, never pack
questions.explanation: The decision follows what you can change: frozen encoder → tile around it; trainable encoder → pack natively (SigLIP 2 NaFlex is the 2025+ open-VLM default); full control → positional scheme does the work.
